heading: Compromise
label: 2677 Compromise
text: The Attorney General or his designee may arbitrate, compromise, or settle any claim cognizable under , after the commencement of an action thereon.section 1346(b) of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 934 (, ).Aug. 2, 1946, ch. 753, § 41360 Stat. 845Changes were made in phraseology.
Senate Revision AmendmentThis section was renumbered “2676” by Senate amendment. See 80th Congress Senate Report No. 1559.

Amendments1966— struck out provision requiring that approval of court be obtained before Attorney General could arbitrate, compromise, or settle a claim after commencement of an action thereon.Pub. L. 89–506

Effective Date of 1966 AmendmentAmendment by applicable to claims accruing six months or more after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 89–506section 10 of Pub. L. 89–506section 2672 of this titleJuly 18, 1966
